metadata.schema_note: Custom schema for academic deliverable. Loosely aligned with STIX 2.1 indicator categorization; not strictly STIX-compliant.

kill_chain_summary.exploitation: User opens .xlsb and enables macros; XLM auto_open formula at $BA$13 fires
kill_chain_summary.installation: Macro retrieves intermediate JavaScript droppers (Fattura.js → lista.js) which fetch and execute block.dll via rundll32
kill_chain_summary.command_and_control: block.dll establishes outbound HTTP(S) C2 to app.buboleinov.com (34.95.142.247) with encoded URI payloads
kill_chain_summary.actions_on_objectives: Banking trojan operations: browser hooking, form grabbing, credential exfiltration via encoded HTTP URIs

file_indicators.description: Original phishing email with malicious .xlsb attachment; spoofed reply thread, SPF FAIL, MailBee.NET X-Mailer, default Windows Message-ID host.
Excel Binary Workbook with XLM (Excel 4.0) macros auto_open at cell $BA$13; uses random-character function names (DFJDFJDF, FDJDFJKERJKJKER) to defeat automated deobfuscation; DocuSign-themed lure.

Core URSNIF DLL payload. 32-bit i386 PE DLL. Statically linked MSVC CRT. Dynamic API resolution via GetProcAddress (15+ COMPUTED_CALL sites). Custom obfuscated arithmetic key derivation in FUN_0103320c (called from Pape1). No commercial packer; runtime decryption of all strings/config.
Secondary stage payload downloaded by URSNIF from staging server. Hash not available in lab (URSNIF could not establish C2 due to DNS sinkhole limitation in lab environment).

c2_domains.description: Primary URSNIF C2 endpoint. Confirmed by both live detonation in the FlareVM lab and the provided PCAP.
Secondary C2 host. Receives encoded URI payloads (likely exfiltration channel).
Staging host that serves /presentation.dll (secondary stage payload).

behavioral_dns_indicators.description: Legitimate Microsoft service queried by URSNIF as a 'do I have internet' check before attempting C2. NOT a malicious indicator on its own; useful as a behavioral correlation signal.
Secondary Microsoft connectivity check. Same caveat as api.msn.com.
URSNIF queries this via OpenDNS to discover its public-facing IP (anti-NAT detection).
OpenDNS resolver used for myip.opendns.com query.

c2_ip_addresses.description: Primary URSNIF C2. Hosted on Google Cloud — common URSNIF tactic to use cloud reputation for IP-reputation evasion.

benign_referenced_ips.description: OpenDNS public DNS resolver. Used by URSNIF for myip.opendns.com query. Not malicious; not actionable.

http_uri_patterns.description: Secondary stage payload download from staging server.

URSNIF C2 communication URI structure. Long URL-safe-base64-style segments separated by URL-encoded delimiters (_2B = '+', _2F = '/'). Carries encrypted C2 payload (config request, beacon, exfiltration).
Cover-traffic requests interspersed with C2 communication. Mimics normal browser behavior.

user_agent_strings.user_agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; WOW64; Trident/7.0; rv:11.0) like Gecko
user_agent_strings.description: URSNIF C2 traffic spoofs IE 11. Note: also used by legitimate IE 11 clients; correlate with destination host.

user_agent_strings.user_agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:86.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/86.0
user_agent_strings.description: URSNIF C2 traffic spoofs Firefox 86. Observed against the same C2 host as the IE 11 spoof — multiple browser identities from a single host is a behavioral fingerprint.

process_indicators.description: URSNIF execution command. Pape1 is a non-standard DLL export name and any rundll32 invocation targeting a non-system DLL with non-standard exports should be treated as suspicious.

process_indicators.process_pattern: rundll32.exe spawning rundll32.exe child, both subsequently exiting
process_indicators.description: URSNIF self-spawn-and-exit pattern observed in detonation. Indicates process injection into a benign host process followed by loader cleanup.

registry_persistence_observations.note: URSNIF made zero RegSetValue operations during 5-minute lab detonation. This is consistent with URSNIF's documented memory-resident execution model. Real-world infections may differ if URSNIF migrates into a process with persistence requirements or operates over longer dwell times.

filesystem_observations.note: URSNIF made zero WriteFile operations during 5-minute lab detonation. Memory-resident operating model confirmed.

yara_rules_authored.match_basis: PDB path, distinctive export combination (Pape1+Riverslow), debug GUID

yara_rules_authored.matches_against: Generic — any PE with section vsize 16x or more larger than raw size
yara_rules_authored.match_basis: Heuristic for runtime unpacking buffer pattern
ZIP central directory filename entries (xl/xld/, xldsheet, binaryIndex)
Obfuscated XLM function identifiers + DocuSign-themed lure strings

mitre_attack_mapping.evidence: Malspam email with .xlsb attachment delivered the infection.

mitre_attack_mapping.technique: T1059.005 — Command and Scripting Interpreter: Visual Basic / Excel 4.0 Macros
mitre_attack_mapping.evidence: XLM auto_open formula at cell $BA$13 in I8m7XluZbbj10J53.xlsb.

mitre_attack_mapping.technique: T1218.011 — System Binary Proxy Execution: Rundll32
mitre_attack_mapping.evidence: block.dll executed via rundll32.exe with non-standard export Pape1.
capa identified Shared Modules technique; runtime DLL loading via LoadLibraryA.
block.dll uses inline runtime decryption with custom obfuscated arithmetic (FUN_0103320c). XLSB uses random-character XLM function names.
block.dll has 15+ GetProcAddress COMPUTED_CALL sites; minimal name-based imports; capa flagged 'link many functions at runtime'.
rundll32.exe self-spawn-and-exit pattern observed; capa identified 'execute shellcode via indirect call'.
capa identified geographical-location capability; .data section contains country-name strings (united-states, united-kingdom, trinidad & tobago).
Rapid QueryDirectory operations against C:\Windows\System32 observed during detonation.
Sequential RegOpenKey/RegQueryKey on COM/OLE registry tree (HKCR\WOW6432Node\CLSID\).

mitre_attack_mapping.technique: T1071.001 — Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
mitre_attack_mapping.evidence: HTTP-based C2 to app.buboleinov.com, todo.faroin.at via long encoded GET URIs.
Uses api.msn.com, go.microsoft.com for connectivity checks; uses myip.opendns.com for public-IP discovery.
Primary C2 hosted on Google Cloud (34.95.142.247); staging server hosted on Bluehost (162.241.24.47). Uses cloud-reputation services for evasion.
